structured.description: A `Shader` class that loads and manages shaders for a 3D graphics pipeline. It uses the OpenGL API to create and link program, uniforms, and matrices. The class provides methods to bind the program, get the program location, uniform float values, and matrix4 values. Additionally, it has a static method `loadShader` that loads a shader from a file and creates a new shader object.

items.description: in Java provides a binding function for a program and uniform and matrix functions. It also loads shaders from files and validates the program.
items.name: Shader

items.description: glides the program object to the current GL context, enabling its use for rendering.
items.name: bind

items.description: returns the value of the `program` field of the current object.

returns.description: an integer representing the program value.

usage.description: In this case, the program variable is assigned the value returned by the getProgram() method, which in this instance is an integer representing the program that was created and initialized with the values passed through the loadShader() method.

items.name: getProgram

items.description: updates a uniform value in a shader program based on the length of the input float array. It calls `glUniformf` with the correct parameter count and passes the location of the uniform and the values to be updated.

params.name: name
params.description: name of the uniform location in the GL context.

usage.description: In this example, the `Shader` class is being used to create a new shader program by loading vertex and fragment shaders from files. The `bind()` method is then called on the shader to use it in the current rendering context. Finally, the `uniformf()` method is being used to set a uniform value for a variable named "myUniform" with the values {1.0f, 2.0f, 3.0f, 4.0f}.

items.name: uniformf

items.description: sets a 4x4 uniform matrix value using `glUniformMatrix4()` with the specified location and transpose status.

params.name: name
params.description: name of the uniform location to which the matrix is being applied.

params.name: mat
params.description:
4x4 floating-point matrix to be uniformed.

The `Matrix4f` class represents a 4x4 matrix with floating-point elements, and the `getLinearData()` method returns a buffer holding the raw memory contents of the matrix. The `Util.createFlippedBuffer()` method reverses the row order of the matrix, which is necessary for GL uniform binding. Finally, the `GL20.glUniformMatrix4()` function sets the specified matrix as a uniform variable in the current GL program.

items.name: unifromMat4

items.description: retrieves the uniform location for a specified name within a program's uniform buffer using the `GL20` class and its `glGetUniformLocation` method.

params.name: name
params.description: name of the uniform location to be retrieved.

returns.description: an integer representing the location of a uniform in a GPU program.

items.name: getLoaction

items.description: loads a shader program from a file and validates its syntax, creating a new shader object that represents the program.

params.name: filename
params.description: name of the file containing the vertex and fragment shaders to be loaded by the `loadShader()` method.

returns.description:
a new `Shader` object representing a shader program created by combining a vertex shader and a fragment shader.

* The output is a `Shader` object, which represents a shader program that can be used for graphics rendering.
* The `Shader` object has a `program` field that contains the handle of the created program.
* The `program` handle is an integer value generated by the OpenGL library, and it is used to identify the shader program in subsequent operations.
* The `loadShader` function creates two separate shader modules (vertex and fragment shaders) and combines them into a single program using the `GL20.glCreateProgram()` function.
* The vertex and fragment shaders are loaded from files specified by the `filename` parameter using the `getText()` function, which reads the contents of a file as a string.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: creates a new shader and loads its source code from a string parameter, then compiles it and attaches it to a program.

params.name: target
params.description: type of shader being created, with a value of `GL_VERTEX_SHADER` for vertex shaders and `GL_FRAGMENT_SHADER` for fragment shaders.

params.name: src
params.description: 1:1 correspondence of source code and shader code to be compiled together in one shader program.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D graphics program that the loaded shader will be attached to.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: validates a program object and prints any errors to the console if they occur.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D program to be validated and linked, which is passed through the `glLinkProgram()` and `glValidateProgram()` functions for validation and linking.

items.name: validateProgram

items.description: reads the contents of a file and returns its text content as a string.

params.name: file
params.description: path of a file that contains the text to be read.

returns.description: a string representation of the contents of the specified file.

items.name: getText
